name: UAC Bypass - Scheduled Task Configured to Run with Highest Privileges
notes: This rule triggers when a scheduled task is configured to run with the highest privileges.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5000872) Success Audit: A scheduled task was created, (5000876) Success Audit: A scheduled task was updated
when the event matches "Run Level" = 'HighestAvailable' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY UAC Bypass - Scheduled Task Configured to Run with Highest Privileges on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5000872) Success Audit: A scheduled task was created, (5000876) Success Audit: A scheduled task was updated
AND when the event matches "Run Level" = 'HighestAvailable' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Possible UAC Bypass - A Scheduled Task Has Been Configured to Run with Highest Privileges
Event description: Possible UAC Bypass - A Scheduled Task Has Been Configured to Run with Highest Privileges
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
